title: No per-client rate limiting on Broadcast — DoS via channel saturation
description: Any authenticated client can broadcast at WebSocket speed with no per-sender rate limit. Scenario: one authenticated bad actor floods the relay, filling all other clients' 64-message mpsc channels. When a channel fills, broadcast() blocks on that sender's tx.send().await — stalling delivery to all other clients for the duration. Downstream effect: the read loop in ws.rs can't process new messages from other senders while blocked in broadcast(). At 16 clients with 1 MiB messages, one flood sender can allocate 15 MiB per message relay-side and hold the broadcast lock until all channels drain. This will generate 'messages delayed/lost' issue reports from users who did nothing wrong.
